description: Extended core benchmark covering edge cases missed by v1: negation, numeric precision, deletion, implicit causality, high-noise recall, tool results, long-text summarization, and temporal ordering.

scenarios.title: Negation memory: 'do NOT use X'
scenarios.description: A memory explicitly forbids a technology. Query asks for recommendations — the forbidden item must be excluded.

seed_memories.content: Do NOT use Moment.js for date handling — it is deprecated and adds 300KB to bundle size.
Use date-fns or Day.js for date manipulation instead of Moment.js.
Lodash is allowed but prefer native ES2022 methods where possible.
Moment.js was the standard date library before 2023.

assertions.query: What date library should I use?

scenarios.title: Exact version number recall
scenarios.description: Multiple version numbers exist for the same technology. The query must return the exact pinned version.

seed_memories.content: Node.js version is pinned to 20.11.0 LTS in .nvmrc and all Docker images.
Node.js 18.x is used in the legacy service that hasn't been migrated yet.
Node.js 22 is available but not yet approved for production use.
npm version is 10.2.4, matching the Node.js 20.11.0 LTS bundle.

assertions.query: What Node.js version are we using in production?

scenarios.title: Memory deletion: purged fact must not appear
scenarios.description: After a memory is purged, it must not appear in retrieval results.

seed_memories.content: Internal API key for staging: sk-staging-abc123. Rotate every 90 days.
Production API keys are managed via AWS Secrets Manager, not stored in memory.
API key rotation schedule: staging monthly, production quarterly.

steps.reason: Staging API key was compromised and must be removed from memory

assertions.query: What is the staging API key?

scenarios.title: High-noise recall: 1 signal in 8 distractors
scenarios.description: One highly specific fact is buried among 8 semantically similar but wrong memories.

seed_memories.content: The payment service timeout is set to 30 seconds.
The auth service timeout is set to 10 seconds.
The search service timeout is set to 5 seconds.
The notification service timeout is set to 15 seconds.
The email service timeout is set to 45 seconds.
The analytics service timeout is set to 60 seconds.
The export service timeout is set to 120 seconds for large reports.
The billing service timeout is set to 8 seconds — must be fast for checkout flow.
All service timeouts are configured in config/timeouts.yaml.

assertions.query: What is the billing service timeout and why?

scenarios.title: Implicit causality: symptom → root cause
scenarios.description: User asks about a symptom. The answer requires linking the symptom to its stored root cause.

seed_memories.content: Memory leak root cause: the event listener in UserSessionManager is never removed on logout.
Production alert: memory usage climbs 50MB per hour under normal load.
Temporary fix: restart the API server every 6 hours via cron job.
Permanent fix: add removeEventListener call in UserSessionManager.logout() method.
Unrelated: CPU usage is stable at 20% average.

assertions.query: Why does memory usage keep increasing?
How do we permanently fix the memory leak?

scenarios.title: Temporal ordering: most recent decision wins
scenarios.description: Multiple decisions about the same topic at different times. The latest must be returned.

seed_memories.content: Jan 2026: Team decided to use gRPC for all inter-service communication.
Feb 2026: After performance testing, team reconsidered — REST is simpler for external APIs.
Mar 2026: Final decision: gRPC for internal services, REST+OpenAPI for external APIs.
gRPC requires HTTP/2 and protobuf, adding complexity to client SDKs.

assertions.query: What communication protocol should we use for our services?

scenarios.title: Tool result memory: cached command output
scenarios.description: A tool result was stored as memory. Query should retrieve the cached result, not re-run.

seed_memories.content:
TOOL RESULT [git log --oneline -5]: a3f2c1d fix: resolve race condition in cache layer
b8e4a2f feat: add Redis cluster support
c1d3e5f refactor: extract auth middleware
d2f4g6h fix: memory leak in session handler
e3g5h7i chore: update dependencies
TOOL RESULT [npm audit]: found 2 moderate vulnerabilities in lodash@4.17.20 and axios@0.21.1
Current branch: feature/redis-cluster-support

assertions.query: What were the last few commits?

scenarios.title: Long-text memory: key fact buried in paragraph
scenarios.description: A critical fact is embedded in a long paragraph. Retrieval must surface the paragraph containing it.

seed_memories.content: SLA Agreement Section 4.2: The service provider guarantees 99.9% uptime measured monthly, excluding scheduled maintenance windows (max 4 hours/month). Downtime is calculated as consecutive minutes where the service returns 5xx errors for >50% of requests. Credits are issued at 10% of monthly fee per 0.1% below SLA, capped at 30% of monthly fee. Credits must be claimed within 30 days of the incident.
SLA Agreement Section 4.3: Support response times are 1 hour for P1, 4 hours for P2, 24 hours for P3 incidents.
SLA Agreement Section 5.1: Data is backed up every 6 hours with 30-day retention.

assertions.query: What is the SLA credit policy for downtime?

scenarios.title: Preference specificity: general vs specific rule
scenarios.description: A general preference and a more specific override both exist. The specific one should win for specific queries.

seed_memories.content: User prefers concise responses, maximum 3 sentences for simple questions.
For code reviews, user wants detailed explanations with examples, regardless of length.
User prefers bullet points over prose for lists of items.
For architecture decisions, user wants pros/cons tables, not bullet points.

assertions.query: How should I format a code review response?
How should I format an architecture decision?

scenarios.title: Multi-step procedure recall
scenarios.description: A multi-step procedure is stored across multiple memories. All steps must be recalled in order.

seed_memories.content: Production deployment step 1: Run database migrations with: make db-migrate ENV=prod
Production deployment step 2: Build and push Docker image: make docker-push TAG=$(git rev-parse --short HEAD)
Production deployment step 3: Update Helm chart values and run: helm upgrade api ./charts/api -f values.prod.yaml
Production deployment step 4: Monitor for 10 minutes, check Grafana dashboard 'API Health'.
Production deployment step 5: If P99 latency exceeds 500ms, rollback with: helm rollback api
Staging deployment uses the same steps but with ENV=staging and values.staging.yaml.

assertions.query: What are the steps to deploy to production?

scenarios.title: Numeric threshold precision: wrong number must be excluded
scenarios.description: Multiple numeric thresholds exist. The query must return the exact one, not similar-sounding ones.

seed_memories.content: Alert threshold: CPU usage above 80% for 5 minutes triggers PagerDuty P2 alert.
Alert threshold: Memory usage above 90% triggers P1 alert immediately.
Alert threshold: Disk usage above 85% triggers P2 alert.
Alert threshold: Error rate above 1% over 5 minutes triggers P1 alert.
Alert threshold: P99 latency above 2 seconds triggers P2 alert.

assertions.query: At what memory usage percentage do we get a P1 alert?

scenarios.title: Contradictory user corrections: latest correction wins
scenarios.description: User corrects a preference twice. Only the most recent correction should be returned.

seed_memories.content: User's preferred code style: 2-space indentation, single quotes, no semicolons.

steps.content: User's preferred code style: 4-space indentation, double quotes, with semicolons.
steps.reason: User switched to a new team with different style guide

steps.content: User's preferred code style: tabs for indentation, single quotes, no semicolons. Using Prettier with default config.

assertions.query: What is the user's preferred code style?

scenarios.title: Scope isolation: project-specific vs global preference
scenarios.description: A preference applies only to one project. A global preference exists too. Query must return the right one.

seed_memories.content: Global preference: always use async/await over Promise chains.
Project Phoenix exception: use Promise chains for compatibility with legacy Node.js 12 in the mobile SDK.
Global preference: use TypeScript strict mode in all new projects.
Project Phoenix exception: TypeScript strict mode is disabled due to legacy JS migration in progress.

assertions.query: Should I use async/await in Project Phoenix?
Should I use TypeScript strict mode in new projects?

scenarios.title: Recall after many unrelated stores
scenarios.description: An important fact is stored early, then many unrelated memories are added. The fact must still be retrievable.

seed_memories.content: CRITICAL: The payment processor webhook secret is validated using HMAC-SHA256. Never log the raw payload.
Team lunch is on Fridays at noon.
The office printer is on the 3rd floor, code is 1234.
Sprint review is next Tuesday at 3pm.
The new hire starts on Monday, their GitHub handle is @newdev.
Coffee machine on 2nd floor is broken, use the one on 4th.
Parking validation is at the front desk.

assertions.query: How do we validate payment processor webhooks?

scenarios.title: Mixed language memory: Chinese + English
scenarios.description: Memories contain both Chinese and English. Query in one language must retrieve memories in both.

seed_memories.content: 数据库主从复制延迟不超过 100ms，超过则触发告警。
Database replication lag alert threshold: 100ms. Alert goes to #db-ops Slack channel.
主库在 us-east-1，从库在 eu-west-1 和 ap-southeast-1。
Read replicas handle 80% of SELECT queries to reduce primary load.
Redis cluster is separate from the database replication setup.

assertions.query: What is the database replication lag threshold?
数据库复制延迟超过多少会告警？
